title: Binder error in macro with subquery
description:
### What happens?

See twin issue https://github.com/duckdb/duckdb/issues/17156 and originating discussion https://github.com/duckdb/duckdb/discussions/16995 .

### To Reproduce

```sql
create or replace macro foo(x) as (
    select apply([x], z->y) as tmp from (select 1 as y)
);
select foo(range) from range(1) 
```
```
 Binder Error: Referenced table "unnamed_subquery" not found!
Candidate tables: "range"
```

### OS:

Linux

### DuckDB Version:

'1.3.0-dev2485'

### DuckDB Client:

Python

### Hardware:

.

### Full Name:

Soeren Wolfers

### Affiliation:

key_bug_features: Binder error with macro using unnamed subquery
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_table (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, value VARCHAR, created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP);
INSERT INTO test_table (id, value) VALUES (1, 'test1'), (2, 'test2'), (3, NULL);
CREATE OR REPLACE MACRO example_macro(x) AS (SELECT apply([x], z->value) AS result FROM (SELECT value FROM test_table WHERE id = 1));
SELECT example_macro(value) FROM test_table WHERE id = 2;
